commands.name: c[lienttypes]
commands.description: Imports editor and formatter types from TypeScript to C#.

commands.name: doctor
commands.description: Analyzes the project and tooling environment.

commands.name: m[vc]
commands.description: Generates IntelliSense helpers for esm entry points and view locations.

commands.name: mvct
commands.description: Executes client types and mvc commands simulatenously.

commands.name: s[ervertypings]
commands.description: Imports row, form, and service types from C# to TypeScript.

commands.name: t[ransform]
commands.description:
Executes clienttypes, mvc, and servertypings commands simultaneously.
Switches:
-p <ProjectFile>  Specifies the project file. Useful when multiple projects exist
in the current directory.
-prop:<n>=<v>     Provides hints to sergen for project-level properties,
where <n> is the property name and <v> is its value.
Use a semicolon to separate multiple properties or specify each
property separately. This is helpful for improving performance
as Sergen won't have to parse the project, and also for addressing
cases where sergen might not determine a property correctly.
